_comment_v3_rewrite: V3 (2026-05-14): full voice rewrite from character brief. Every line re-drafted against the five-test Taste Standard. Maritime metaphors limited to ~1-in-4 and reserved for panic beats only. Composed-then-collapsing sentence shape. Specific objects (rent envelope, printer lease, chair). No state-announcing, no maritime decoration, no stacked traits. Address forms verified: Cula = 'Dr. A. Cula', Murrow = 'Mr. Murrow', Sikorska = 'Mrs. Sikorska'. Coffee reactions rewritten for voice without changing gate logic. idle_flavor varied between low-grade managed worry and quiet beats.
_comment_v1_4_coffee: V1.4 (2026-05-12): two coffee_reaction_* states. Gated on chapter1.coffee_buff and chapter1.met_pig per the design brief. Additional clause !chapter1.entered_court so coffee reactions stop firing once the court scene begins. Priority placement BEFORE has_binder_and_memo preserved.

lines: It would appear that we are currently —

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Mr. Pig. Good morning. Dr. A. Cula.

lines: Ah — Dr. A. Cula. Welcome. Have you met the rent envelope. We've been having a long conversation, the envelope and I.
Sit, please. Yes — that chair. The other one is — no. That one.

Let me explain the firm's position. The firm is not bankrupt. Bankruptcy has paperwork. We are in the phase that comes before the paperwork, which is the phase where one stares at the envelope and considers whether opening it constitutes acceptance.
The rent is six weeks overdue. The landlord has sent a letter. He sends it monthly. The tone is evolving.
There is also the printer lease, which has lapsed, and which I have been renewing by telephone every Thursday, and which the leasing company has stopped answering the telephone about, which is new.
Mr. Swine is abroad. On business. The nature of the business is — well. The postcard said Sapporo. I didn't know we had clients in Sapporo. I don't believe we do.

This brings me to Mrs. Sikorska. She is being evicted on Friday. She should not be. The procedure is wrong. The address on the notice is wrong. Everything about it is wrong, Dr. A. Cula, except that she will lose her apartment on Friday unless someone files a motion by then.
Mr. Murrow has the file. Mr. Murrow has — he has the whole thing. Ask Mr. Murrow.
Right. Right. Yes. Welcome to Pig & Swine.

states.line: Mr. Murrow. Down the hall. He is the one who looks like he has read everything in the building and found most of it wanting.
The procedural binder is in the archive. Blue spine. Second shelf. If it is not on the second shelf, it is on the floor near the second shelf. There is a distinction and I respect it.
The binder. Good. Half a file is half a case. The other half is — somewhere. I am told it exists.

states._comment: V3 coffee reaction — S-grade. Pig sees the coffee and his eye goes to its commercial potential. Composed-then-collapsing shape. Address form correct.
states.line: Dr. A. Cula. That coffee. Is that — can we serve that to clients? No. Can we charge for it. That's the question. That is always the question.

states._comment: V3 coffee reaction — D/F-grade. The coffee reminds him of a cost. Specific object, not a general complaint.
states.line: Dr. A. Cula. I have been looking at that coffee and I have been looking at the printer lease and the colour is — I want to say similar. I want to say that and then move on.
The binder and the memo. Both in the same building at the same time. This is — this is how firms operate, Dr. A. Cula. In theory. I have read about it.

idle_flavor: The chair is creaking again. I had it looked at. The man said it was structural. I asked him not to use that word in this building.
If anyone needs me I am here. At the desk. With the envelope.
